description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages (build and deploy) and global variables PATH and PIP_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES_OPTION, applying common rules that skip any branch matching ^windows.* and otherwise always run. In the build stage define a build-sdist job using the python:3 image, running apt-get update and installing swig, libssl-dev, python3-dev, python3-pip, python3-build, openssl, python3-setuptools, python3-twisted, then executing the common setup script (mkdir -p $HOME/.local/bin $CI_PROJECT_DIR; touch .env_setup; export PATH), sourcing .env_setup, installing dev-requirements with pip, and building an sdist via "python -mbuild . --sdist", and publishing dist/*.tar.gz as an artifact. Also in the build stage create fedora, fedora-rawhide, leap, opensuse, python3, python3-32bit, python3-doctest, and python39 jobs each using their respective images (quay.io/fedora/fedora:latest, quay.io/fedora/fedora:rawhide, opensuse/leap, opensuse/tumbleweed, python:3, i386/debian with linux32 entrypoint, python:3.9-alpine), installing development tools via dnf, zypper, apt or apk as appropriate, then running the common setup script, the common python build script (pip install $PIP_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES_OPTION --user -r dev-requirements.txt; python -mpip wheel ...; install the built wheel into the discovered build lib directory; export BUILD_LIB_DIR and PYTHONPATH), and the common tests script (source .env_setup; python -mdoctest -v src/M2Crypto/SSL/Checker.py; python -munittest discover -b -v tests); fedora-rawhide is marked allow_failure, leap overrides PIP_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES_OPTION to empty, and all jobs extending .irc_notification send an IRC message after the script. The python3-doctest job additionally runs pip install -r doc/requirements.txt and executes "PYTHONPATH=$(readlink -f build/lib.*) make -C doc/ doctest" after sourcing the environment. Finally, in the deploy stage add a release-pypi job using python:latest that runs only on tags matching a semantic version (manual trigger), installs jq, upgrades pip and installs twine and id, obtains an OIDC token for PyPI, mints a PyPI API token via curl, and uploads all files in dist/* to PyPI with twine.
